activity.title: How AI Is Ushering in the Next Era of Risk Review at Meta
activity.raw_text: Meta has integrated AI into the core of its Risk Review program, helping teams address potential privacy, safety, and security concerns before launch faster and more accurately. This program surfaces relevant legal requirements, prefills relevant documentation, and catches potential product issues during the product development process, serving as an always-on risk detection tool. Using AI in the risk review process strengthens expert decision-making and ensures consistent application of safety and privacy standards.

_verify_phrases: integrated AI into the core of its Risk Review program

enrichment.summary: Meta published a blog post describing its integration of AI into its internal Risk Review program. The AI-assisted program surfaces legal requirements, prefills documentation, and detects potential privacy, safety, and security issues during product development. Meta frames the tool as strengthening expert decision-making and ensuring consistent application of safety and privacy standards before product launches.
enrichment.stance_quote: Using AI in the risk review process strengthens expert decision-making and ensures consistent application of safety and privacy standards.

source_entity.name: Meta
source_entity.description: Operator of the Llama open-weights model family — the central case study in open-source AI policy debates. Yann LeCun is the loudest industry voice against catastrophic-risk framings. Faces concurrent antitrust (FTC Instagram/WhatsApp), Section 230, and EU DMA pressure that bleeds into AI questions.
